introSection:
Mr. Freeze is a steel launched shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. It is a prototype Shuttle Catapult Coaster built by Premier Rides. The ride is a mirror image of the version at Six Flags St. Louis.

At the time of its opening in 1998, the ride held the record for the third tallest, longest and second fastest shuttle coaster. It also featured the highest inversion on a roller coaster at.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The roller coaster was originally planned to be built at Six Flags AstroWorld in Houston, Texas, USA.

On December 18, 1996, Six Flags Over Texas announced that Mr. Freeze would be added to the park. Mr. Freeze was originally planned to open in May 1997 to coincide with Batman & Robin's release. However, it suffered launch problems, causing the ride's opening to be delayed to 1998.

Mr. Freeze officially opened on March 28, 1998. A soft opening was held four days prior on March 24.

When the ride first opened, it had over-the-shoulder restraints. In 2002, they were replaced with lap bars.

In 2007, Mr. Freeze was repainted with a red track and dark blue supports.

For the 2012 season, the trains were reversed, meaning that the ride now launched riders backwards.

During the 2021 season, the attraction was repainted light blue, its original color scheme.

The ride closed on September 11, 2022 for a refurbishment. A few weeks later, it was announced that the ride would have its trains restored to the original forwards direction. This also saw the name of the ride being reverted to Mr. Freeze.

One train was returned to the reverse-facing direction in summer 2023.

On February 11, 2026, the park announced that ride's queue would receive a complete refurbishment with new scenes and effects.

sections.title: Ride experience
sections.content: The ride features a switchback, allowing for two trains to run. The ride launches guests from 0-70 mph in 3.8 seconds using an LIM Launch. Following that, riders experience a 150 foot tall inverted top hat. Immediately, riders will go through a 113 foot tall 140 degree over-banked curve. They will then go up a 218 foot tall vertical spike. To help the ride gain enough speed to complete the course forwards and backwards, there is an LIM boost that pulls the train higher on the spike, and then thrust it down. The riders then traverse the course again forwards.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On April 10, 2022, several guests were evacuated from the main ride building when an electrical malfunction caused smoke to overflow the interior. Seven people were treated for smoke and five others were sent to a local hospital.

fullText:
Mr. Freeze is a steel launched shuttle roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Texas in Arlington, Texas, USA. It is a prototype Shuttle Catapult Coaster built by Premier Rides. The ride is a mirror image of the version at Six Flags St. Louis.

At the time of its opening in 1998, the ride held the record for the third tallest, longest and second fastest shuttle coaster. It also featured the highest inversion on a roller coaster at.

The roller coaster was originally planned to be built at Six Flags AstroWorld in Houston, Texas, USA.

On December 18, 1996, Six Flags Over Texas announced that Mr. Freeze would be added to the park. Mr. Freeze was originally planned to open in May 1997 to coincide with Batman & Robin's release. However, it suffered launch problems, causing the ride's opening to be delayed to 1998.

Mr. Freeze officially opened on March 28, 1998. A soft opening was held four days prior on March 24.

When the ride first opened, it had over-the-shoulder restraints. In 2002, they were replaced with lap bars.

In 2007, Mr. Freeze was repainted with a red track and dark blue supports.

For the 2012 season, the trains were reversed, meaning that the ride now launched riders backwards.

During the 2021 season, the attraction was repainted light blue, its original color scheme.

The ride closed on September 11, 2022 for a refurbishment. A few weeks later, it was announced that the ride would have its trains restored to the original forwards direction. This also saw the name of the ride being reverted to Mr. Freeze.

One train was returned to the reverse-facing direction in summer 2023.

On February 11, 2026, the park announced that ride's queue would receive a complete refurbishment with new scenes and effects.



The ride features a switchback, allowing for two trains to run. The ride launches guests from 0-70 mph in 3.8 seconds using an LIM Launch. Following that, riders experience a 150 foot tall inverted top hat. Immediately, riders will go through a 113 foot tall 140 degree over-banked curve. They will then go up a 218 foot tall vertical spike. To help the ride gain enough speed to complete the course forwards and backwards, there is an LIM boost that pulls the train higher on the spike, and then thrust it down. The riders then traverse the course again forwards.



On April 10, 2022, several guests were evacuated from the main ride building when an electrical malfunction caused smoke to overflow the interior. Seven people were treated for smoke and five others were sent to a local hospital.
